subject: [PATCH v3 15/28] nfsd: use RCU to protect fi_deleg_file

2026-04-28.patch_summary: This patch addresses a concurrency issue in the NFS server code by using RCU (Read-Copy Update) to protect access to the fi_deleg_file pointer. The problem arises when put_deleg_file() nulls out the pointer, and concurrent accesses from workqueue callbacks can dereference a NULL pointer if they race with this teardown. To fix this, the patch annotates fi_deleg_file with __rcu and converts all accessors to use RCU primitives, such as rcu_assign_pointer() for stores and rcu_dereference_protected() for reads under certain conditions. This prepares the ground for a subsequent patch that will safely acquire a reference from the CB_NOTIFY callback path without holding fi_lock.
